themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are overwhelmingly won over by the ice cream quality and the Liège waffle combination, with Belgium comparisons appearing unprompted; the friction, when it surfaces, is specific and consistent: a sample-limit policy that isn't posted anywhere, communicated poorly in the moment.
quarter_narrative: Treat spent Spring 2026 doing what it has always done well: earning near-unanimous five-star reviews on the strength of a genuinely specific product, Liège waffles and small-batch ice cream in a Shadyside storefront that photographs well. Six hundred and twenty-three Google reviews at 98 percent five-star is a number that speaks for itself. The Instagram account, though, has been silent for roughly 200 days, and no Pittsburgh TikTok creators have indexed the business yet, which means the visual energy showing up in 202 Google photos has nowhere to travel beyond the platform it lands on. In a Sweets family where Page's is setting the pace on social momentum, Treat's path from Ones to Watch into the upper tier runs directly through restarting that feed and giving creators a reason to walk in with a camera.
tldr_read: Six hundred-plus reviews and a perfect conversion path, but a dormant Instagram and zero TikTok presence keep it at Ones to Watch, seventh in Pittsburgh Sweets.
tldr_meaning: Every foundational signal is in place; the rank climbs when the social layer, silent for nearly seven months, comes back online and the creator ecosystem discovers the product.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Seventh In Sweets, Carried By 623 Reviews. The Feed Has Been Quiet For Seven Months.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram feed now
playbook.action: The account has posted zero times in 200 days; even two posts a week would reactivate a 7.65 percent engagement rate that is already well above average.

playbook.headline: Post the sample policy before a customer does
playbook.action: Two separate reviews name the same friction point; a small in-store sign and one Instagram post explaining the sample limit removes the recurring one-star risk.

playbook.headline: Build a taggable visual moment for creators
playbook.action: No Pittsburgh TikToks are indexed yet; a waffle-plus-scoop beauty shot at the counter, with a tagged location, gives the first creator a clear entry point.
